paper_title: Genotype Analysis of Transgenic and Wild-Type Plant DNA

methods.method_name: Genomic DNA Extraction from Plant Tissue
methods.description: Process for isolating genomic DNA from plant cotyledons using a series of physical and chemical steps including tissue disruption, lysis, centrifugation, organic extraction, alcohol precipitation, and washing. This method is used to obtain purified DNA for subsequent genotype verification by PCR.

steps.description: Cotyledons with size of 1 cm wide and 2 cm long were excised
steps.searchable_summary: Excise plant tissue (cotyledons)

steps.description: placed into a 1.5 mL tube
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer excised tissue to a tube

steps.description: followed by immediate immersion into liquid nitrogen
steps.searchable_summary: Flash freeze tissue sample using liquid nitrogen

steps.description: before storage.
steps.searchable_summary: Store frozen tissue samples

steps.description: Genomic DNA was isolated from the frozen samples by crushing with a tooth pick
steps.searchable_summary: Mechanically disrupt frozen tissue by crushing

steps.description: and 1 mL of the extraction solution was added
steps.searchable_summary: Add extraction buffer to crushed tissue
inputs: Extraction solution (0.35 M glucose, 0.1 M Tris-HCl, 50 mM Na2EDTA, 2% PVP, 1% β-Me, ddH2O)

steps.description: before tubes were centrifuged at 10,000 ×g for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge sample at 10,000 xg for 10 minutes

steps.description: The supernatant was discarded
steps.searchable_summary: Discard the supernatant

steps.description: and the pellet was added to a 600 µL lysis solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Add lysis buffer to the pellet
inputs: Lysis solution (1.4 M NaCl, 0.1 M Tris-HCl, 20 mM Na2EDTA, 2% CTAB, 2% PVP, 1% β-Me, ddH2O)

steps.description: The lysis process was conducted using incubating at 65 °C for 30 min turning over every 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate sample at 65°C for 30 minutes with mixing

steps.description: To separate DNA from the dissolved protein or other materials, the solution was then added to a 600 µL solution mixture consisting of chloroform and isoamyl alcohol with a proportion of 24:1.
steps.searchable_summary: Add chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1) for organic extraction

steps.description: The solution was shaken 30 times
steps.searchable_summary: Mix sample by shaking

steps.description: and centrifuged at 11,000 ×g for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge sample at 11,000 xg for 10 minutes for phase separation

steps.description: The DNA-containing resultant solution was transferred (500 µL) into a new tube
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer the aqueous phase (upper layer) to a new tube

steps.description: to which 500 µL of chloroform in isoamyl alcohol (24:1) was added.
steps.searchable_summary: Add chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1) for a second organic extraction

steps.description: After mixing and centrifuging at 11,000 ×g for 10 min
steps.searchable_summary: Mix and centrifuge sample at 11,000 xg for 10 minutes for second phase separation

steps.description: the DNA, upper layer, was then transferred (400 µL) into a new tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer the aqueous phase (upper layer) to a new tube after second extraction

steps.description: To precipitate the DNA, the solution was then added to 40 µL sodium acetate
steps.searchable_summary: Add sodium acetate to the aqueous phase

steps.description: and 240 µL isopropanol.
steps.searchable_summary: Add isopropanol to the solution

steps.description: The solution was kept at -20 °C for 15 min or continued immediately to be centrifuged at 11,000 ×g for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Precipitate DNA by chilling at -20°C or immediate centrifugation

steps.description: The supernatant solution obtained was then discarded
steps.searchable_summary: Discard the supernatant after DNA precipitation

steps.description: and added 1 mL ethanol (70%).
steps.searchable_summary: Add 70% ethanol to wash the DNA pellet

steps.description: Thereafter, the solution was mixed
steps.searchable_summary: Mix the DNA pellet with ethanol

steps.description: and centrifuged at 12,000 ×g for 3 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge sample at 12,000 xg for 3 minutes for ethanol wash

steps.description: The precipitated DNA was washed by using ethanol (70%) repeated three times
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat ethanol wash (steps 22-24) three times

steps.description: and dried for 5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry the washed DNA pellet

steps.description: The purified DNA was then added 50 µL ddH2O for smaller DNA or 100 µL ddH2O for bigger DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: Resuspend purified DNA pellet in ddH2O

steps.description: The DNA was kept at -20 °C or used immediately to check its quality
steps.searchable_summary: Store purified DNA at -20°C or use immediately
outputs: Stored DNA OR DNA ready for use

methods.description: Procedure for verifying the presence or absence of the INVINH1 gene in plant genomic DNA using Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) with specific primers. Includes setup for positive and negative controls.

steps.description: PCR was conducted using INH primers designed using PRIMER-BLAST from NCBI.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up PCR reaction using purified DNA and INH primers

steps.description: The primary pair obtained from the program was forward primer: TGTTGGTAGAGCCATTGTAAGA and primary reward: GGGGATACACACATAACATTTGAGG, with each TM 57.1 for forward primer and 59.7 for primary reward
steps.searchable_summary: Define forward and reverse primer sequences and melting temperatures (Tm)

steps.description: and the size of the expected product was 200 bp.
steps.searchable_summary: Note the expected size of the PCR product (amplicon)

steps.description: The primer will detect the INVINH1 of genomic DNA of wild type and control plants, which encodes a protein inhibitor of the cell wall invertase enzyme.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe the target gene (INVINH1) detected by the primers

steps.description: The control plant was used for positive control in that the plants that had been known the gene.
steps.searchable_summary: Include a positive control using DNA from a plant known to contain the target gene

steps.description: In addition, negative control were used using the primer added without genomic DNA (or water) in the mixture.
steps.searchable_summary: Include a negative control using primers and reagents without template DNA (using water instead)
